project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 12 - Admin UI Polish: Sidebar Branding, Standard Filters, Enhanced PII Display
sprints.goal: Three UI improvements: (1) Change sidebar header from 'Navigation' to 'LLM Gateway'; (2) Replace audit page filter inputs with standard shadcn/ui components; (3) Enhance Log Detail drawer to show PII type details with counts and expandable sections.

features.title: Change sidebar header from 'Navigation' to 'LLM Gateway'
features.description: In admin/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx, change the sidebar header text from 'Navigation' to 'LLM Gateway'. This is a simple text change in the collapsed/expanded header area.
acceptance_criteria: Given the sidebar is expanded, when user views the header, then they see 'LLM Gateway' instead of 'Navigation'
Given the sidebar is collapsed, when user views the header area, then no text is shown (current behavior preserved)
features.technical_notes: Single line change: replace 'Navigation' with 'LLM Gateway' in the span element at line 51-52 of sidebar.tsx.

features.title: Install shadcn/ui and add DatePicker, Select components
features.description: Set up shadcn/ui in the admin project: (1) Install @radix-ui/react-popover, @radix-ui/react-select, date-fns dependencies; (2) Create admin/src/lib/utils.ts with cn() helper if not exists; (3) Add DatePicker component (Popover + Calendar); (4) Add Select component. Configure components.json for shadcn CLI compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: Given shadcn/ui is installed, when running pnpm dev, then no dependency errors occur
Given DatePicker component exists, when imported, then it renders a calendar popover
Given Select component exists, when imported, then it renders a styled dropdown
features.technical_notes: Follow shadcn/ui installation guide for Vite + Tailwind. Create components in admin/src/components/ui/. Use existing clsx and tailwind-merge dependencies. DatePicker needs date-fns for date formatting.

features.title: Replace audit page filter inputs with shadcn/ui components
features.description: In admin/src/pages/audit.tsx, replace the native HTML date inputs and select elements with shadcn/ui DatePicker and Select components. Maintain the same filter functionality (startDate, endDate, model, status) but with consistent styling.
acceptance_criteria: Given the audit page loads, when viewing filters, then DatePicker components replace native date inputs
Given the audit page loads, when viewing filters, then Select components replace native dropdowns
Given a date is selected via DatePicker, when the value changes, then the filter updates and table refreshes
Given a model/status is selected via Select, when the value changes, then the filter updates and table refreshes
features.technical_notes: Use shadcn/ui DatePicker with Popover. Model and status filters use Select with 'All models'/'All statuses' placeholder options. Maintain existing state management (startDate, endDate, modelFilter, statusFilter).

features.title: Enhance Log Detail drawer to show detailed PII information
features.description: In admin/src/pages/audit/detail-drawer.tsx, enhance the PII display section. Currently it only shows 'Yes (EMAIL, PHONE)'. Enhance to: (1) Show PII types as a list with badges; (2) Show count of each PII type detected; (3) Add an expandable section showing the mapping structure (placeholder → type) for transparency. Note: actual PII values are not stored for security.
acceptance_criteria: Given a log with pii_detected=true, when viewing Log Detail, then PII types are shown as individual badges
Given a log with multiple PII types, when viewing Log Detail, then the count of each type is shown
Given a log with pii_detected=false, when viewing Log Detail, then 'No' is shown (current behavior)
Given the PII section, when user wants more details, then an expandable section shows type breakdown
features.technical_notes: Parse pii_types_found JSON array. Create a collapsible/expandable section using a simple button + conditional render (or use shadcn/ui Collapsible if available). Show types like: 'EMAIL: 2 detected', 'PHONE: 1 detected'. Note: actual values cannot be shown as they're not persisted.

features.title: Run typecheck and build to verify all changes
features.description: After implementing all features, run pnpm build in admin/ directory and pnpm typecheck at root to ensure no TypeScript errors. Fix any issues that arise.
acceptance_criteria: Given all features implemented, when running pnpm build in admin/, then build succeeds with 0 errors
Given all features implemented, when running pnpm typecheck at root, then 0 errors
features.technical_notes: Run: cd admin && pnpm build && cd .. && pnpm typecheck. This validates the frontend changes compile correctly.

sprints.name: Phase 11 - Add /api Prefix to All Backend API Routes
sprints.goal: Unify all backend API routes under /api prefix (/api/v1/* for OpenAI-compatible routes, /api/admin/* for admin routes), fix existing V1 prefix doubling bug, update all tests and frontend API client. /health, /docs, and /admin/ static serving remain unchanged.

features.title: Restructure route registration in index.ts with /api prefix scope
features.description: In src/index.ts: (1) Remove the existing { prefix: "/v1" } from V1 scope registration — this fixes the prefix doubling bug where production routes become /v1/v1/chat/completions. (2) Wrap both V1 routes and admin route plugins inside a new { prefix: "/api" } scope. The V1 scope stays as a nested scope for hook isolation (auth, rate-limit, security) but with no prefix. Admin plugins (keys, audit, config) move inside the /api scope alongside V1. serveAdminPlugin stays outside the /api scope (static files at /admin/).
acceptance_criteria: Given the restructured registration, when server starts, then /api/v1/chat/completions resolves correctly (not /v1/v1/chat/completions)
Given the restructured registration, when server starts, then /api/admin/keys resolves correctly
Given the restructured registration, when server starts, then /health and /docs remain at root level
Given the restructured registration, when server starts, then /admin/ static assets still serve at /admin/ (not /api/admin/)

features.title: Update audit logger URL filter and serve-admin plugin
features.description: Two changes: (1) In src/audit/logger.ts, change the URL filter from startsWith("/v1/") to startsWith("/api/v1/") so audit logs are only generated for V1 proxy routes. (2) In src/plugins/serve-admin.ts, remove the API_PREFIXES array and isApiRoute() function. Since API routes now live under /api/admin/* (not /admin/*), there is no longer a conflict between API routes and static asset routes — the onRequest hook and upgrade handler can simply check !request.url.startsWith("/api") instead of maintaining a prefix list.
acceptance_criteria: Given a request to /api/v1/chat/completions, when onResponse fires, then an audit log entry is created
Given a request to /api/admin/keys, when onResponse fires, then NO audit log entry is created (only /api/v1/ is audited)
Given dev mode, when /admin/ is requested (non-API, non-/api), then it proxies to Vite
Given dev mode, when /api/admin/keys is requested, then it is NOT proxied to Vite (handled by Fastify)
features.technical_notes: For serve-admin.ts: replace the isApiRoute() check with !request.url.startsWith("/api"). The API_PREFIXES array and isApiRoute function become unnecessary. The rest of the proxy logic stays the same.

features.title: Update test helpers (setup.ts and admin-server.ts) with /api prefix
features.description: Update both test server setup files to match the new route registration structure: (1) tests/helpers/setup.ts — remove the { prefix: "/v1" } from V1 scope (or don't add one — the current test setup doesn't have it, just confirm it stays without prefix scope), then wrap V1 routes + admin plugins in { prefix: "/api" }. (2) admin/e2e/fixtures/admin-server.ts — same restructure. Also update the seedAuditLog default endpoint from "/v1/chat/completions" to "/api/v1/chat/completions".
acceptance_criteria: Given updated setup.ts, when tests run, then /api/v1/chat/completions resolves in test server
Given updated setup.ts, when tests run, then /api/admin/keys resolves in test server
Given updated admin-server.ts, when E2E tests run, then /api/v1/chat/completions resolves
Given updated admin-server.ts, seedAuditLog default endpoint is /api/v1/chat/completions
features.technical_notes: setup.ts currently has NO prefix on V1 scope (routes use their own /v1/ prefix). After change: wrap everything in { prefix: "/api" }, keep V1 scope without prefix. admin-server.ts has the same structure. Note: current test URLs in tests already use /v1/chat/completions (not /v1/v1/...) because tests don't add the prefix scope.

features.title: Update all unit tests for new /api prefix paths
features.description: Update URL paths in unit test files: (1) src/routes/v1/chat-completions.test.ts — all /v1/chat/completions → /api/v1/chat/completions. (2) src/routes/v1/embeddings.test.ts — all /v1/embeddings → /api/v1/embeddings. (3) src/routes/v1/models.test.ts — all /v1/models → /api/v1/models. (4) src/plugins/serve-admin.test.ts — rewrite tests that check API route detection: /admin/keys is no longer an API route in the serve-admin sense (API routes are now /api/admin/keys), so /admin/keys should be proxied to Vite in dev mode. Update assertions accordingly. (5) src/db/audit-store.test.ts — endpoint strings like "/v1/chat/completions" → "/api/v1/chat/completions".
acceptance_criteria: Given updated tests, when pnpm vitest run src/routes/v1/ passes, then all V1 unit tests pass with /api/v1/ URLs
Given updated serve-admin.test.ts, when tests run, then /admin/keys is treated as a non-API route (proxied to Vite in dev mode)
Given updated serve-admin.test.ts, when tests run, then /api/admin/keys is recognized as API route (not proxied)
Given updated audit-store.test.ts, when tests run, then endpoint assertions use /api/v1/ prefix
features.technical_notes: For serve-admin.test.ts this is a significant change — the concept of 'API route' in serve-admin changes from 'URLs matching /admin/keys, /admin/audit, etc.' to 'URLs starting with /api'. The test for 'does NOT proxy /admin/keys' should become 'DOES proxy /admin/keys' since it's no longer an API route in the serve-admin context. Add new test: 'does NOT proxy /api/admin/keys'.

features.title: Update all integration tests for new /api prefix paths
features.description: Update URL paths in all integration test files under tests/integration/. Every occurrence of /v1/ in request URLs becomes /api/v1/. Every occurrence of /admin/ in request URLs (for API calls, not browser navigation) becomes /api/admin/. Also update endpoint assertion values (e.g., expect(log.endpoint).toBe("/api/v1/chat/completions")). Files: audit.test.ts, admin-keys.test.ts, admin-config.test.ts, openapi.test.ts, security.test.ts, pii-redact.test.ts, metrics.test.ts, retry-failover.test.ts, models.test.ts, rate-limit.test.ts, streaming.test.ts, hooks.test.ts, auth.test.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Given updated tests, when pnpm vitest run tests/integration/ passes, then all integration tests pass with /api/ prefixed URLs
Given updated openapi.test.ts, when spec is fetched, then paths include /api/v1/chat/completions, /api/v1/embeddings, /api/v1/models, /api/admin/keys, etc.
Given updated audit.test.ts, when audit log endpoint is asserted, then it equals /api/v1/chat/completions
Given updated retry-failover.test.ts, when audit log endpoint is asserted, then it equals /api/v1/chat/completions
features.technical_notes: This is the largest change by file count. Use search-and-replace carefully — only replace URL paths in inject() calls and endpoint assertions. Do NOT change browser navigation URLs in E2E tests (those are SPA routes, not API routes). OpenAPI spec test will need special attention — the path keys in the spec will now be /api/v1/... and /api/admin/...

features.title: Update frontend API client BASE_URL to include /api prefix
features.description: In admin/src/lib/api-client.ts, change the BASE_URL default from "" to "/api". This way all frontend API calls (which use paths like /admin/keys, /admin/audit/logs, etc.) will resolve to /api/admin/keys, /api/admin/audit/logs, etc. No changes needed in individual hooks — they all use apiClient.get/post/patch/del with relative paths that will be appended to /api.
acceptance_criteria: Given updated api-client.ts, when frontend calls apiClient.get("/admin/keys"), then the request goes to /api/admin/keys
Given updated api-client.ts, when VITE_API_BASE env var is set, then it overrides the default /api prefix
features.technical_notes: Change: const BASE_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE ?? "/api";. All hooks already use paths like "/admin/keys", "/admin/audit/logs", etc. — these become "/api/admin/keys", "/api/admin/audit/logs" when prepended with BASE_URL. No other frontend files need changes.

features.title: Update openapi plugin descriptions and E2E test fixtures
features.description: Two minor updates: (1) src/plugins/openapi.ts — update security scheme description strings from '/v1/* endpoints' to '/api/v1/* endpoints' and from '/admin/* endpoints' to '/api/admin/* endpoints'. (2) admin/e2e/fixtures/index.ts — if it contains any URL paths that reference API endpoints, update them. The E2E browser navigation URLs (/admin/keys, /admin/audit, etc.) are SPA routes and should NOT change.
acceptance_criteria: Given updated openapi.ts, when /docs is accessed, then security scheme descriptions reference /api/v1/* and /api/admin/*
Given E2E tests, when browser navigates to /admin/keys, then the SPA page loads correctly (route unchanged)
features.technical_notes: This is a minor cleanup feature. The openapi.ts descriptions are just documentation strings. E2E browser URLs are client-side SPA routes served from /admin/ static files — they don't go through /api prefix.

features.title: Final verification: typecheck + full test suite pass
features.description: After all features are implemented, run the full verification pipeline: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test. This ensures all 646+ tests pass with the new /api prefix. Fix any remaining issues. This is a validation gate, not implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given all features implemented, when pnpm typecheck runs, then 0 errors
Given all features implemented, when pnpm test runs, then all tests pass (0 failures)
features.technical_notes: Run: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test. This should be the last step. If tests fail, investigate and fix — likely missed URL path references in test files.
